Title: The Hobbit
Author: Tolkien, J.R.R.
My Review: A timeless journey into Middle-Earth. Every page is a delight.

Title: Dune
Author: Herbert, Frank
My Review: Deepest science fiction I've ever read. The political intrigue and ecology were fascinating.

Title: Murder on the Orient Express
Author: Christie, Agatha
My Review: Classic Christie — the twist ending left me speechless.

Title: The Name of the Wind
Author: Rothfuss, Patrick
My Review: Beautiful prose. Kvothe's story sucked me in completely.

Title: Foundation
Author: Asimov, Isaac
My Review: The grand sweep of history is masterfully done.

Title: The Silent Patient
Author: Michaelides, Alex
My Review: Finished it in one sitting. Unputdownable.

Title: Pride and Prejudice
Author: Austen, Jane
My Review: Austen's wit is unmatched. I laughed out loud more times than I can count.

Title: Neuromancer
Author: Gibson, William
My Review: Gritty, prophetic, and still feels fresh decades later.

Title: The Dragon Republic
Author: Kuang, R.F.
My Review: Harrowing and brilliant. RF Kuang writes war like no one else.

Title: Circe
Author: Miller, Madeline
My Review: A luminous reimagining of Greek myth. Circe's voice stayed with me for weeks.

Title: Project Hail Mary
Author: Weir, Andy
My Review: Rocky! The most joyful sci-fi I've read in years. Pure optimism.

Title: The Poppy War
Author: Kuang, R.F.
My Review: Brutal and unforgettable. Kuang's willingness to go there sets her apart.

Title: The Shadow of the Wind
Author: Zafon, Carlos Ruiz
My Review: A love letter to books themselves. Barcelona never felt so alive.

Title: Shogun
Author: Clavell, James
My Review: Immersive historical epic — 1000+ pages flew by.

Title: The Three-Body Problem
Author: Liu, Cixin
My Review: Mind-bending first contact story. Chinese cultural context was refreshing.

Title: Pachinko
Author: Lee, Min Jin
My Review: A sweeping multi-generational saga. Deeply human.

Title: The House in the Cerulean Sea
Author: Klune, T.J.
My Review: Like a warm hug in book form. The found-family narrative is exquisite.

Title: The Maidens
Author: Michaelides, Alex
My Review: Good atmosphere but the twist felt slightly forced compared to his debut.

Title: The Maid
Author: Prose, Nita
My Review: Gentle mystery. The neurodivergent protagonist perspective was interesting.

Author: Orwell, George
My Review: Haunting and still terrifyingly relevant. Everyone should read this.
